title: Criminal Intimidation
text: Criminal intimidation covers threats to person, reputation, or property intended to cause alarm or compel a person to do or omit an act. Digital threats, abusive messages, and coercive demands may attract this provision depending on the facts and evidence.

title: Cheating and Dishonest Inducement
text: Cheating involves deception coupled with dishonest inducement that causes delivery of property or valuable security. Online scams, fraudulent investment schemes, and misrepresentation for wrongful gain may trigger this section when intent is proved.

title: Outraging Modesty and Sexual Harassment
text: Sexual harassment can include unwelcome physical contact, sexually coloured remarks, demands for sexual favours, or showing pornography. The factual matrix, witness testimony, and electronic evidence are critical for assessing liability.

title: Defamation
text: Defamation concerns making or publishing an imputation harming the reputation of a person. Messages, posts, and circulated content may amount to defamation unless a legal exception such as truth for public good applies.

title: Electronic Records
text: Electronic records can be admitted in evidence when accompanied by the statutory certificate under Section 65B, subject to judicial interpretation. Device details, chain of custody, and metadata preservation materially affect admissibility.

title: Breach of Contract Remedies
text: Compensation for breach of contract is generally limited to losses arising naturally in the usual course of things or contemplated by the parties. Penalty and liquidated damages clauses are scrutinized for reasonableness.
